articles.title: FIDELITY LAUNCHES TOKENIZED TREASURY FUND ON ETHEREUM
articles.summary: Fidelity has quietly launched the Fidelity Digital Interest Token (FDIT) on Ethereum, a tokenized version of its Treasury money market fund that has already accumulated over $200 million in assets since beginning operations in August. The move puts Fidelity in direct competition with BlackRock's BUIDL fund, which dominates the tokenized Treasury market with over $2 billion in assets under management, as part of a broader $7 billion tokenized Treasury ecosystem that includes offerings from Franklin Templeton and WisdomTree. Despite currently having only two holders, FDIT marks another major traditional finance player adopting blockchain infrastructure to improve market efficiency, reduce settlement times, and cut costs. McKinsey analysts project that tokenized securities could reach $2 trillion in market value by the end of the decade.

articles.title: COULD A DOGECOIN ETF BE LAUNCHED THIS WEEK?
articles.summary: The Rex-Osprey DOGE ETF ($DOJE) is expected to launch this week, becoming the first U.S. exchange-traded fund to provide direct exposure to Dogecoin's price movements. The ETF is backed by REX Shares and Osprey Funds, the same firms behind the Solana staking ETF that launched earlier this year, and represents the latest expansion of crypto ETF offerings following the approval of spot Bitcoin and Ethereum products in 2024. DOGE has rallied 7% in the past 24 hours and 15% over the week in anticipation of the launch, outperforming major cryptocurrencies as market participants expect increased volatility and potential speculative inflows. The launch comes amid a broader race among issuers to create ETF products tied to alternative cryptocurrencies, with Canary Capital also recently filing for a TRUMP coin ETF in late August.

articles.title: NATIVE USDC AND CCTP V2 COMING TO HYPERLIQUID
articles.summary: Circle announced that native USDC and its upgraded Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol (CCTP V2) will soon be live on Hyperliquid's HyperEVM. This integration enables direct USDC deposits into HyperCore, institutional-grade on/offramps via Circle Mint, and seamless use across HyperEVM apps. With CCTP V2, developers can build efficient crosschain apps for swaps, treasury rebalancing, and onboarding, positioning USDC as a key settlement asset for Hyperliquid's trading and DeFi ecosystem.

articles.title: ETHEREALIZE'S NEXT CHAPTER
articles.summary: Etherealize is building institutional-grade infrastructure on Ethereum, including zero-knowledge privacy tools, settlement engines, and tokenized fixed income applications. The company was co-founded by former Ethereum Foundation research lead Danny Ryan and Wall Street veteran Vivek Raman, with a team combining Ethereum core developers and executives from Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and other major financial institutions. Since launching in January, Etherealize has engaged with hundreds of banks and asset managers, collaborated on bringing new tokenized assets onchain, and testified before Congress on digital asset regulation. There is growing institutional momentum around Ethereum, which now processes 95% of stablecoin volume and 82% of tokenized real-world assets, positioning it as the dominant platform for institutional blockchain adoption.

articles.title: THE BIDDING WAR FOR USDH ON HYPERLIQUID
articles.summary: Hyperliquid's upcoming native stablecoin, USDH, has drawn competing proposals from Paxos, Frax, and Agora, each offering different approaches to compliance, yield sharing, and ecosystem integration. Paxos leans on regulatory clarity and institutional bridges like PayPal, and Frax emphasizes zero-fee DeFi-native efficiency with frxUSD backing, while Agora pledges 100% net revenue sharing through a coalition model with partners like Rain and LayerZero. The outcome, chosen by validator vote, will shape how Hyperliquid transitions away from its current heavy reliance on USDC.

articles.title: HYPERLIQUID: THE BLOCKCHAIN TO HOUSE ALL FINANCE
articles.summary: Hyperliquid isn't just the largest decentralized perp exchange, it's building the foundation to house all of finance. Legacy banking runs on outdated 40+ year-old tech stacks like COBOL, leaving institutions fragmented and opaque. Hyperliquid instead offers a unified chain with deep liquidity, builder incentives, and HIP-3 permissionless markets. With 279+ projects already building via Builder Codes, a forthcoming native stablecoin (USDH), on/off-ramps, and gasless transfers, Hyperliquid could become the global settlement layer for everything from spot trading to payments, positioning $HYPE as core infrastructure for the next trillion-dollar opportunity.

articles.title: USDH STABLECOIN CUSTODIAN SCRUTINY
articles.summary: Bridge's reliance on a small regional bank (with a <$2B balance sheet) is a scaling risk for Hyperliquid's planned USDH stablecoin. With $5B already in USDC deposits on Hyperliquid, custodians need GSIB-level infrastructure (e.g., State Street with $49T assets) to support growth. Smaller banks face regulatory limits when deposits expand too quickly, which can halt scaling and damage customer confidence.

articles.title: TEMPO'S ARGUMENT FOR L1 FINALITY
articles.summary: Tempo should be a standalone L1, not an Ethereum L2, because real-world payments require sub-second finality that Ethereum (15m) and Bitcoin (60m) can't provide. Since most rollups inherit Ethereum's long finality window, they aren't viable for physical goods or services that can't be reversed. By contrast, self-settled rollups (like those built with Sovereign SDK on Celestia) already hit ~6s finality and are expected to drop to ~300ms, making them better suited for payment rails.

articles.title: REVENUE TO TOKEN HOLDERS INCREASING
articles.summary: Over the past year, more protocols have been sharing larger portions of revenue with their token holders, with the biggest distributors being Pump, Hyperliquid, and Aerodrome.

articles.title: ETHEREUM VALIDATOR FLOWS FLIP BACK TO GROWTH
articles.summary: The ETH validator entry queue has overtaken the unstaking queue, signaling renewed demand for staking.

articles.title: STABLECOINS: FROM REGULATORY ARBITRAGE TO MARKET REALITY
articles.summary: Stablecoins do not only thrive because of regulatory arbitrage.
